status_definitions.implemented: Available through a tested Linux-native path, subject to any stated qualification scope.
status_definitions.partial: A useful tested subset is available, with explicit missing behavior.
status_definitions.in_progress: Implementation is active on Stage 3 but is not yet an enabled end-to-end product path.
status_definitions.planned: Suitable for a Linux-native implementation but not yet present.
status_definitions.not_portable: Depends on a Windows-only provider or filesystem with no verified Linux equivalent.
status_definitions.deferred: Deliberately outside the current critical path and not promised for the next release.

features.upstream_feature: Create bootable drives from disk images, including compressed and virtual-disk images
features.notes: Raw, ISOHybrid, ZIP, gzip, bzip2, XZ, LZMA, Zstandard, VHD, VHDX, QCOW2, and VMDK inputs use source/target identity checks, cancellation, flush, and optional physical-device verification.
GPT/MBR, UEFI, x86 BIOS/CSM, FAT32, NTFS, WIM splitting, UEFI:NTFS, setup options, and driver staging are implemented through Linux-native tooling.

features.upstream_feature: Customize Windows Setup and OOBE behavior
features.notes: Hardware-check bypass, offline/local account, privacy, regional settings, time zone, BitLocker suppression, and driver loading are implemented. Rufus 4.15 Quality of Life policy is under exact-head review in PR #290. Silent installation, SkuSiPolicy deployment, and Windows CA 2023 bootloader selection remain separate high-risk/media-policy tranches.

Guarded GPT/UEFI creation, verified writable-tree copy, identity-bound GUI flow, and reboot qualification are available for qualified Ubuntu casper and Debian live-boot media. Broader distribution-specific support remains planned.

features.upstream_feature: Perform runtime validation of UEFI bootable media
features.notes: Descriptor-rooted UEFI structural and Secure Boot analysis, Rufus-compatible md5sum manifests, full-tree verification, a reproducibly built ARM64 validation loader, transactional installation, and QEMU chainload qualification are implemented. Physical hardware coverage remains a release-qualification item.

features.upstream_feature: Compute MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, and SHA-512 checksums
features.notes: All four Rufus-compatible image checksum algorithms are available in one descriptor-bound pass. MD5 and SHA-1 are explicitly excluded from trust decisions.

features.upstream_feature: Perform bad-block checks, including fake flash-drive detection
features.notes: Windows-media workflows provide a destructive write/read qualification option. Rufus-style multi-pattern passes, wraparound/fake-capacity detection, and reusable qualification reports remain planned.

features.upstream_feature: Create DD/VHD, VHDX, FFU, and ISO images of an existing drive
features.notes: Identity-bound raw drive-image backup with progress, cancellation, SHA-256, atomic publication, and GTK integration is complete. Sparse VHD/VHDX export, UDF ISO capture, and FFU capture remain unimplemented Linux-native formats.
Explicitly deferred as a later maybe. It is not on the Stage 3 critical path and requires Linux-native deployment, BCD/offline-registry work, and extensive physical qualification.

features.upstream_feature: Download official Microsoft Windows and UEFI Shell images
features.notes: Resumable verified acquisition, threshold-signed metadata, rollback-resistant cache, GUI workflow, and offline administration tools exist. Production offline-key ceremony and signed catalog publication remain pending.
FAT32, NTFS, and ext4 persistence paths are implemented. exFAT, UDF, and selected ext workflows remain planned. ReFS has no verified Linux-native formatter and is not portable today.

features.upstream_feature: Create FreeDOS or MS-DOS bootable drives
features.notes: Deterministic FreeDOS 1.4 media creation is complete. MS-DOS binary acquisition and licensing-dependent creation are not implemented.

features.upstream_feature: Read, write, and capture Full Flash Update images
features.notes: Linux-native read-only inspection, descriptor planning, chunk hash verification, catalog-member binding, PKCS#7 signature verification, and an inactive explicit Authenticode trust-bundle foundation are implemented. Chain/publisher trust, authenticated root activation, loop restoration, privileged restore, GTK flow, and hardware qualification remain disabled. Upstream Rufus delegates FFU operations to Windows DISM/FfuProvider and therefore does not supply a portable executor.

features.upstream_feature: Provide UEFI:NTFS boot support across firmware architectures
features.notes: Verified x86, x86-64, and ARM64 UEFI:NTFS assets are integrated. Upstream Rufus 4.15 adds RISC-V64 support, which remains planned and is outside current ARM64 qualification.

features.upstream_feature: Extract limited El Torito UEFI images for firmware and nonstandard optical media
features.notes: El Torito and optical boot structures are inspected and reported, but upstream-compatible UEFI image extraction is not yet implemented.
GTK strings and diagnostics must move into a translation catalog before language packs are accepted.

features.upstream_feature: Small portable executable and updateable distribution
features.notes: The Debian package is reproducible and includes privileged helpers and the WIM engine. AppImage or another audited portable format, signed release artifacts, and verified updates remain planned.

features.upstream_feature: Dark mode, tooltips, keyboard navigation, and accessible UI
features.notes: GTK keyboard navigation, assistive names, selectable diagnostics, resizable layout, and system-theme behavior exist. Complete option tooltips, explicit light/dark selection, and translation-aware accessibility review remain planned.
